content.decision_question: Should the state extend binding anti-segregation regulation into free school choice and selective admissions despite maintainer autonomy objections and anticipated political resistance, or limit intervention to domains with less institutional pushback even if segregation channels remain structurally unaddressed?
content.evidence_boundary: Evidence on segregation patterns, referral thresholds, and legal precedent can document the scale and mechanisms of sorting and predict likely resistance intensity. It cannot determine how much weight to give parental choice and maintainer autonomy relative to desegregation obligations, since this is a normative balance, not a factual question.
content.tension: Extending anti-segregation safeguards to free school choice and selective gimnázium tracks (TP-live-t6) directly targets documented unaddressed sorting mechanisms and responds to unresolved ECtHR/EU obligations, but doing so requires new legal authority over admissions and provider autonomy (public, private, church), domains with a decade-plus record of institutional resistance and low likelihood of durable enactment absent external enforcement.
content.title: Anti-segregation coordination versus institutional and maintainer autonomy
